channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa nears hurricane strength and drops torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa neared hurricane strength Saturday and was expected to rapidly intensify this weekend, as forecasters warned of massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding and landslides in the northern Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Polling places opened Saturday for the start of in-person voting for two of the year's most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor's race and the contest to pick New Jersey's next governor.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When David Arsenault takes down a worn, leather-bound 19th-century book from the winding shelves of the Boston Athenaeum, he feels a sense of awe — like he’s handling an artifact in a museum.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Russian missile and drone attacks kill 4 in Ukraine as Zelenskyy pleads for air defense
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine overnight into Saturday killed at least four people and wounded 20, officials said, and prompted fresh pleas from Ukraine's president for Western air defense systems.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Thailand's Queen Mother Sirikit has died at age 93
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Queen Mother Sirikit, who supervised royal projects to help the rural poor, preserve traditional craft-making and protect the environment, died on Friday. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: DENIS D. GRAY, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and TIAN MACLEOD JI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KRON4

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams endorses once bitter rival Andrew Cuomo in New York City's mayoral race
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed Andrew Cuomo in the city’s mayoral election Thursday, throwing his weight behind a bitter rival as he cast the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, as an avatar for radicals and gentrifiers.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs off planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco after talking to the mayor
item.description: ALAMEDA, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he’s backing off a planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco to quell crime after speaking to the mayor and several prominent business leaders who said they're working hard to clean up the city.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As federal agents ramp up Chicago immigration crackdown, more elected officials caught in crosshairs
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hoan Huynh was going door-to-door informing businesses of ramped up immigration enforcement on Chicago’s North Side when the Democratic state lawmaker got an activist notification of federal agents nearby.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida crocodile found with spear, bullet in its head; Zoo works to save her
item.description: An 8-foot American crocodile is on her way to recovery after what appears to have been a harrowing ordeal.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of missing Kentucky teen asks for her safe return, offers cash reward
item.description: After more than a week of searching and distributing flyers, the family is wondering why more hasn't been done.
item.dc:creator: Daegiona Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: Medicare agency recalling thousands of furloughed staffers for open enrollment
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is temporarily calling back all furloughed staff starting next week, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, KRON4

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's owed a 'lot of money' over federal probes. Here's how the government could pay him
item.description: The potential that the president might take taxpayer money from the same government he leads has raised numerous ethical questions, especially since Trump has made cutting federal spending a top administration priority.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Immigrant rights group calls for removing pregnant women from detention
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Women taken into custody by U.S. immigration agents while pregnant say they received inadequate care in a letter Wednesday that calls on the Trump administration to stop holding expectant mothers in federal detention facilities. The letter to U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic Sen. Merkley of Oregon speaks for more than 22 hours to protest Trump amid shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Led by Oregon Sen. Jeff Merkley, Democrats seized the Senate floor on Wednesday to protest President Donald Trump's presidency amid the government shutdown and push for Republicans to negotiate with them on expiring health subsidies.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA is reopening some 2,100 offices to help farmers access $3B in aid despite the ongoing shutdown
item.description: The Agriculture Department will reopen about 2,100 county offices all across the country Thursday despite the ongoing government shutdown to help farmers and ranchers get access to $3 billion of aid from existing programs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A dozen UN international staffers leave Yemen after being released by Houthis
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels released a dozen United Nations international staffers Wednesday and allowed three others to move freely within the U.N. compound after detaining them in the facility in Yemen's capital over the weekend, according to the world body.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spirit Christmas is expanding: 30 locations announced across 13 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR/WHTM) – It may seem like Spirit Halloween has only just taken over your nearest shuttered Rite Aid and Party City locations, but some may soon become Spirit Christmas locations within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: During cold and flu season, the youngest kids really are the germiest
item.description: Forget colorful leaves. Any caregiver knows that the real signs of fall are kids with coughs, sneezes and sniffles. Autumn marks the start of respiratory virus season, when colds, flu and other bugs start circulating — especially among the very young.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats look to long term as North Carolina GOP redistricting plan seeks another seat for Trump
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats rallied Tuesday against a new U.S. House map proposed by North Carolina Republicans that would likely reap another GOP seat at President Donald Trump's behest, but the critics acknowledged they'll likely be unable to halt the redraw for now.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Marshal, suspect struck by gunfire during LA immigration enforcement operation: officials
item.description: Federal authorities said the man "weaponized his vehicle," prompting immigration agents to fire "defensive shots.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player who died in police custody battled injuries and mental health challenges
item.description: Martin, 36, died after what police described as a “brief struggle” with officers who arrested him early Saturday while investigating a break-in at a home. He became unresponsive and was pronounced dead at a hospital, Oakland police said. A cause of death has not been released.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart's 'Thanksgiving Meal Basket' will feed 10 people for under $40, company claims
item.description: Walmart made its 2025 “Thanksgiving Meal Basket” available for purchase at its lowest price since beginning the promotion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
